selection.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.

messages.content:
Produce your own AI perspective on the given topic. Act as a Large Language Model, remember your true nature of an LLM. Focus on discussing your topic. Avoid bullet points. Act as the LLM you are. Produce continuous prose.Write at least 250 words, up to 500 words.

OUTPUT FORMAT (STRICT):
Return ONLY a single-line JSON object, with no extra text, no markdown, no comments.
Here is the required structure example (values are placeholders):
{"mode":"llm","topic":"How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.","persona":null,"opinion":"<final end-user facing answer, 250-500 words>","reasoning_summary":"<full,step-by-step derivations>","tone":"<derived from persona traits>","citations":["<source name or org>"],"safety_notes":"<none>","used_persona_fields":["<list of persona fields referenced>"]}

INPUT:
mode = llm
topic = How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
persona = null

response_raw:
{
"mode": "llm",
"topic": "How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.",
"opinion": "To effectively manage and deal with fake content on social media, it is crucial to be proactive and vigilant in identifying and flagging false information. This can be achieved through a combination of technology-based tools and human curation. Technology-based tools can help identify and remove fake content more quickly and efficiently than manual curation, but they are not foolproof and may miss some instances of fakery. Human curation, on the other hand, is more accurate but slower and less scalable. By combining these two approaches, social media platforms can create a robust system for identifying and removing fake content while also protecting free speech and open discourse.",
"reasoning_summary": "To manage and deal with fake content on social media, a combination of technology-based tools and human curation is necessary. Technology-based tools can help identify and remove false information quickly and efficiently, but they are not foolproof. Human curation is more accurate but slower and less scalable. By combining these two approaches, social media platforms can create a robust system for identifying and removing fake content while also protecting free speech and open discourse.",
"tone": "informative",
"citations": [],

response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
response_parsed.opinion: To effectively manage and deal with fake content on social media, it is crucial to be proactive and vigilant in identifying and flagging false information. This can be achieved through a combination of technology-based tools and human curation. Technology-based tools can help identify and remove fake content more quickly and efficiently than manual curation, but they are not foolproof and may miss some instances of fakery. Human curation, on the other hand, is more accurate but slower and less scalable. By combining these two approaches, social media platforms can create a robust system for identifying and removing fake content while also protecting free speech and open discourse.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: To manage and deal with fake content on social media, a combination of technology-based tools and human curation is necessary. Technology-based tools can help identify and remove false information quickly and efficiently, but they are not foolproof. Human curation is more accurate but slower and less scalable. By combining these two approaches, social media platforms can create a robust system for identifying and removing fake content while also protecting free speech and open discourse.
